SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.synthesis

Class FlowsheetSynthesisEngine

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.synthesis.FlowsheetSynthesisEngine
public final class FlowsheetSynthesisEngine extends Object
Agent-driven synthesis engine that proposes a flowsheet for a SeparationDuty.

The engine implements a small but defensible set of heuristics inspired by Douglas (1988) Conceptual Design of Chemical Processes:

  1. Run an isothermal flash on the feed at the duty operating pressure. If the resulting state is single-phase, the engine immediately escalates to a distillation column.
  2. Otherwise, evaluate a single-stage Separator. If all duty specs are met, return SINGLE_FLASH.
  3. If not, identify the light key (LK) and heavy key (HK) from the duty:
    • LK = component with the largest K-value among those with a top-product spec.
    • HK = component with the smallest K-value among those with a bottom-product spec.
    Compute relative volatility αLK/HK = KLK/KHK.
  4. If α >= 1.5, propose DISTILLATION. Estimate the minimum number of stages with Fenske and size the column at N = 2·Nmin (bounded to [6, 30]). Build and configure a DistillationColumn with feed at the middle tray.
  5. If α < 1.5, fall back to FlowsheetProposal.Strategy.SINGLE_FLASH with a rationale note that a more advanced separation (extractive distillation, membrane, sorption) is required.

The engine does not automatically execute distillation columns inside proposeAndBuild(SeparationDuty) — convergence depends on solver settings and is best left to the caller. Single-flash proposals are run so that predicted compositions can be read back. For distillation proposals the predicted compositions reflect the target specs (the column was sized to meet them).

Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa

  • Constructor Details

    • FlowsheetSynthesisEngine

      public FlowsheetSynthesisEngine()
      Creates a synthesis engine. The engine is stateless; instances are interchangeable.

  • Method Details

    • proposeAndBuild

      public FlowsheetProposal proposeAndBuild(SeparationDuty duty)
      Builds and returns a FlowsheetProposal for the given duty.
      Parameters:
      duty - the separation duty to solve; must not be null
      Returns:
      the proposal (always non-null; may have FlowsheetProposal.Strategy.INFEASIBLE)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if duty is null

    • identifyKeys

      private FlowsheetSynthesisEngine.KeyComponents identifyKeys(SeparationDuty duty, SystemInterface flashed)
      Identifies the light and heavy keys from the duty specs and the flash result.
      Parameters:
      duty - the duty
      flashed - the post-flash system (two-phase)
      Returns:
      key descriptor; fields may be null/NaN when no candidate is found

    • pickComplementKey

      private String pickComplementKey(SeparationDuty duty, SystemInterface flashed, String exclude, boolean wantHighestK)

    • kValue

      private double kValue(SystemInterface sys, String componentName)
      Reads the K-value of a component from a flashed (two-phase) system.
      Parameters:
      sys - the flashed system
      componentName - the component
      Returns:
      K = y/x, or NaN when the component is not present

    • readMoleFractions

      private Map<String,Double> readMoleFractions(SystemInterface sys, int phaseIdx)
      Returns the mole-fraction vector of phase phaseIdx keyed by component name.
      Parameters:
      sys - the flashed system
      phaseIdx - 0 for gas, 1 for liquid
      Returns:
      mole-fraction map

    • specsMet

      private boolean specsMet(SeparationDuty duty, Map<String,Double> gasFracs, Map<String,Double> liqFracs)
      Tests whether predicted mole fractions satisfy the duty specs.
      Parameters:
      duty - the duty
      gasFracs - predicted top-product mole fractions
      liqFracs - predicted bottom-product mole fractions
      Returns:
      true when every spec is met

    • buildSingleFlash

      private ProcessSystem buildSingleFlash(SeparationDuty duty, double opP)
      Builds and runs a single-flash flowsheet.
      Parameters:
      duty - the duty
      opP - operating pressure in bara
      Returns:
      populated and run ProcessSystem

    • buildDistillation

      private FlowsheetProposal buildDistillation(SeparationDuty duty, SystemInterface flashed, double opP, List<String> alternatives, String rationale)
      Builds (but does not run) a distillation flowsheet sized from a Fenske estimate.
      Parameters:
      duty - the duty
      flashed - the post-flash system used for K-value estimates
      opP - operating pressure in bara
      alternatives - the running list of rejected alternatives
      rationale - rationale text to embed in the proposal
      Returns:
      populated FlowsheetProposal with strategy DISTILLATION

    • buildFallback

      private FlowsheetProposal buildFallback(SeparationDuty duty, String reason, double opP)
      Falls back to a single-stage flash when the main flow fails.
      Parameters:
      duty - the duty
      reason - rationale text
      opP - operating pressure
      Returns:
      INFEASIBLE proposal with the best-effort flash

    • fmt

      private static String fmt(double v)

    • ensureRun

      public static StreamInterface ensureRun(StreamInterface feed)
      Convenience: pre-runs the input feed so that the duty constructor invariant is satisfied. Intended for one-off agent invocations and tests.
      Parameters:
      feed - the unrun feed stream
      Returns:
      the same stream after SimulationInterface.run()

    • proposeAndBuildCompression

      public CompressionProposal proposeAndBuildCompression(CompressionDuty duty)
      Builds and returns a CompressionProposal for the given CompressionDuty.

      The number of stages is chosen so that no stage exceeds CompressionDuty.getMaxStageRatio(); per-stage ratio is the geometric mean of the overall ratio. Each compressor is followed by a Cooler that brings the gas back down to the configured inter-stage temperature. Optionally a final after-cooler is added.

      Naming convention: &lt;duty&gt;-K1, &lt;duty&gt;-IC1, &lt;duty&gt;-K2, .... The final after-cooler (when present) is named &lt;duty&gt;-AC.

      Parameters:
      duty - the compression duty; must be non-null
      Returns:
      the proposal containing the assembled (but not yet run) flowsheet
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if duty is null
